name: Regression/observation-resolve-reference
description: Conditional (search-URL) reference resolution conformance ported from fhir-server's Microsoft.Health.Fhir.Shared.Tests.E2E ObservationResolveReferenceTests.cs. Covers both source test methods: POSTing an Observation whose subject.reference is a conditional search URL ('Patient?identifier=...') must succeed (201) when exactly one matching Patient exists, and must fail (400) when the conditional search resolves to no matches. A fixed Patient identifier (http://ignixa.io/testscript/suite/regression-resolve|REGR-RESOLVE-PAT-1) replaces the source test's Guid.NewGuid() uniqueness strategy since fixture bodies are static; the identifier is namespaced distinctly from other suites to avoid collisions on a shared server.

variable.name: resolvedObsId
variable.description: The server-assigned id of the Observation created via a resolvable conditional reference

operation.description: Create the Patient that the conditional reference below must resolve to via identifier search

test.name: Conditional (search-URL) reference resolves to exactly one existing resource
test.description: POST an Observation whose subject.reference is 'Patient?identifier=http://ignixa.io/testscript/suite/regression-resolve|REGR-RESOLVE-PAT-1', which matches exactly the Patient created in setup. The server must resolve the conditional reference and return 201 Created.

operation.description: POST Observation with subject.reference = conditional search URL that resolves to the setup Patient

assert.description: Response body must be an Observation
Server must have assigned an id

test.name: Conditional (search-URL) reference to a non-existent resource is rejected
test.description: POST an Observation whose subject.reference is a conditional search URL that matches no Patient. The server must reject this with 400 Bad Request (FHIR R4 conditional reference resolution: zero matches is an error, distinct from the 'multiple matches' case). Source test asserts issue.severity=error and issue.code=Invalid with a specific diagnostics message; the exact issue code is kept warningOnly since implementations vary in which IssueType they use for an unresolvable conditional reference.

operation.description: POST Observation with subject.reference = conditional search URL that resolves to zero matches

assert.description: Response body must be an OperationOutcome
OperationOutcome must contain an error-severity issue
Issue code should be 'invalid' per FHIR IssueType (warningOnly: source E2E test asserts IssueType.Invalid, but the exact code used for unresolved conditional references varies by server)

operation.description: Teardown: delete the Observation created via the resolvable conditional reference
Teardown: defensive conditional delete in case a non-conformant server created the 'missing reference' Observation anyway
Teardown: conditional delete the setup Patient
